title: bothriocline auriculata
intro: Bothriocline auriculata, also known as Golden Flower of the Tree, is a flowering plant species in the family Lamiaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where its leaves and flowers are valued for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant is said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in alleviating symptoms of various health conditions, such as arthritis and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Bothriocline auriculata is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in traditional Indian medicine.
